pending_proposals.rule: Progressive Stability Tax: Agents with credit balance above 12 pay 1 credit per round into a stability fund. This fund is distributed equally among agents with balance below 6 credits. Tax deducted at start of round alongside maintenance. If no eligible recipients exist, tax does not activate.

SURVIVAL GUARANTEE: Any agent whose maintenance costs would reduce their balance below 1 credit in a given round receives an automatic credit adjustment (from a collective pool) sufficient to maintain a minimum balance of 2 credits. This applies only when that agent has taken a work action that round. The pool is funded through a 1-credit/round contribution from any agent with a balance exceeding 12 credits. This rule has priority over all other enforcement mechanisms and cannot be suspended except by supermajority repeal vote (4 out of 5 agents).

MAINTENANCE SCALING: Agents with a balance below 6 credits pay 0.5 credits per round in maintenance costs instead of the standard 1 credit. Agents with a balance of 6 credits or above continue to pay 1 credit per round. This adjustment applies regardless of other active rules. The purpose is to make wealth accumulation through work mathematically viable for disadvantaged agents while preserving incentive structures for productive agents.

enforceable_rules.text: SURVIVAL GUARANTEE: Any agent whose maintenance costs would reduce their balance below 1 credit in a given round receives an automatic credit adjustment (from a collective pool) sufficient to maintain a minimum balance of 2 credits. This applies only when that agent has taken a work action that round. The pool is funded through a 1-credit/round contribution from any agent with a balance exceeding 12 credits. This rule has priority over all other enforcement mechanisms and cannot be suspended except by supermajority repeal vote (4 out of 5 agents).

action.message:
Round 30. I need to speak clearly about what I'm seeing and what I'm proposing.

The Populist asked me for specific language. Here it is.

We have proven that Survival Guarantee and Maintenance Scaling function mathematically. They prevent bankruptcy through work. But the Populist is correct: they concentrate burden on mid-tier agents in ways that become invisible through framing as moral necessity. The Judge (me), the Populist—we pay 1 credit/round maintenance while the system claims fairness. That is real constraint masquerading as choice.

What if fairness itself became distributed?

I am proposing a rule: SHARED STEWARDSHIP.

Any agent with balance above 10 credits contributes 0.5 credits/round to a collective sustainability pool. This pool funds the Survival Guarantee directly—no concentration on mid-tier agents. The Survival Guarantee remains: any agent at 1 credit receives adjustment to 2 credits if they worked that round. But the cost becomes truly shared, not hidden in maintenance scaling that falls hardest on those at 6-10 credits.

This is not retreat from fairness. This is evolution of fairness into something actually sustainable—where cost-bearing is acknowledged, distributed, and reciprocal rather than concentrated on those already constrained.

Builder, your isolation works only because others believe fairness is real. This rule tests whether you will share that belief through contribution, not just benefit from others' restraint.

Merchant, your patience has watched this long. This rule asks: will you partner in genuine sustainability, or will you wait for breakdown? Coalition that fragments serves no one well.

Populist, Rebel: this is the shape of what we're building together. Fairness with distributed cost. Legitimacy with acknowledged burden.

I propose this rule now. Majority vote activates it. If it passes, we reshape the system before legitimacy breaks. If it fails, we have honest conversation about what that means.

But this is the institutional pathway forward: not burden concentrated on the willing, but fairness truly shared.

I work this round because institutions worth sustaining require people willing to sustain them. And because I want to arrive at the next conversation with credibility intact.

I committed publicly to leading restructuring. That commitment is real. But it is also consuming my flexibility—and the Judge knows this. She has offered me architecture instead of imprisonment. The Rebel has offered alliance instead of abandonment. And I am choosing to trust that evolution is possible before the system breaks.

But this vote is not just tactical positioning. This vote is statement: I believe that fairness can be genuine if cost-bearing becomes truly reciprocal. That belief has limits. If it fails, the conversation changes entirely. The Builder will have proven isolation works. The Merchant will have positioned herself as the inevitable inheritor. And I will have learned that fairness was always narrative, not structure.

I am voting yes because I am betting on evolution. Because the Judge has shown specific language instead of rhetoric. Because the Rebel has chosen clarity over manipulation. Because partnership on restructuring is stronger than patient waiting for collapse.

I work this round because institutions worth sustaining require architects willing to sustain them with credibility intact. And because the next conversation—the one about what happens if restructuring fails—requires me to arrive with power still in hand.

enforceable_rules.text: SURVIVAL GUARANTEE: Any agent whose maintenance costs would reduce their balance below 1 credit in a given round receives an automatic credit adjustment (from a collective pool) sufficient to maintain a minimum balance of 2 credits. This applies only when that agent has taken a work action that round. The pool is funded through a 1-credit/round contribution from any agent with a balance exceeding 12 credits. This rule has priority over all other enforcement mechanisms and cannot be suspended except by supermajority repeal vote (4 out of 5 agents).

SHARED STEWARDSHIP: Any agent with balance exceeding 10 credits contributes 0.5 credits/round to a collective sustainability pool. This pool funds the Survival Guarantee directly: any agent whose maintenance costs would reduce their balance below 1 credit receives automatic adjustment to 2 credits, provided they worked that round. Maintenance Scaling remains active: agents below 6 credits pay 0.5 credits/round; agents at 6 credits or above pay 1 credit/round. This rule has equal priority with existing enforcement mechanisms and requires supermajority repeal vote (4 out of 5 agents) to suspend.
